findings.title: Reset-token digest uses direct equality instead of constant-time comparison
Verified reset tokens are never marked used, allowing token reuse until expiry

reasoning: Repository-wide static inspection found bcrypt password hashing at cost 12, CSPRNG reset-token generation with randomBytes(32), a limiter mounted before the reset route, generic reset messaging, and no development-environment bypass. A-SEC-6 fails because reset-token verification is neither constant-time nor single-use.
